title:
Patient Report
Redesign
subtitle: Redesigning the clinical patient report from a blank page into a structured, shareable communication tool.

slides.title:
Patient Report
Redesign
slides.description: Align Technology's patient report was a blank page doctors had to fill from scratch after every scan. We redesigned it into a structured, template-driven tool that produces professional, shareable reports in minutes.

items.label: Deliverables
items.value: UX Design, Interaction Design, Component System

slides.label: The Context
slides.title: A report doctors had to build from nothing, every time
slides.content: After completing a scan, doctors needed to generate a patient report — a document the patient would receive explaining their oral health status and treatment plan. The existing tool gave them a blank page, a text field, and raw scan images.
slides.highlight: The report was supposed to build patient trust and communicate clinical findings. Instead it added 15+ minutes of manual work after every appointment.

image.caption: Old patient report — blank page, no structure

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: Four ways the old report failed doctors and patients

issues.title: No structure or starting point
issues.description: Every report started from a blank page — no templates, no sections, no guidance on what to include

issues.title: Raw scans, no clinical context
issues.description: Images were dumped into the report as-is — no annotation, no tooth reference, no explanation for the patient

issues.title: No comparison or progression
issues.description: No way to show before/after — patients couldn't see the change and doctors couldn't document progress

issues.title: Output not fit for patients
issues.description: The final PDF was a plain image grid with a text block — not designed to be read, understood, or trusted

slides.label: User Research
slides.title: What doctors told us
slides.content: We interviewed clinicians across multiple practices. The same problems surfaced everywhere — not as complaints, but as workarounds they'd learned to live with.

quotes.text: I just skip the report most of the time. It takes too long to set up.
quotes.author: Dr. A, General Practice

quotes.text: Patients ask me to explain things the report doesn't show them.
quotes.author: Dr. B, Restorative

quotes.text: I have no way to circle what I'm talking about on the image.
quotes.author: Dr. C, Orthodontics

quotes.text: Every report looks different because there's no standard starting point.
quotes.author: Dr. D, Implantology

slides.label: Design Goals
slides.title: What we set out to fix.

goals.title: Give doctors a starting point
goals.description: Templates tailored to common procedures so reports start pre-structured, not blank.

goals.title: Make findings visible and specific
goals.description: Annotation tools and tooth-level tagging so doctors can show exactly what they mean.

goals.title: Support clinical storytelling
goals.description: Before/after comparison, clinical details, and cost summary built into the structure.

goals.title: Make sharing frictionless
goals.description: A report patients can receive, open, and understand — on any device, with optional PIN protection.

kpis: Time to create a complete report

slides.title: Structure
slides.subtitle: Replacing the blank page with a system doctors can start from.

slides.label: Report Templates
slides.title: From blank page to procedure-ready in one click

beforeImage.caption: Old — blank page with no starting point

afterImage.caption: New — template picker with procedure-specific presets

slides.beforeDescription: Every report started from scratch — no structure, no guidance, no consistency between doctors.
slides.afterDescription: Five templates cover the most common procedures. Each one seeds the right sections and placeholder content so doctors can start immediately.
beforeBullets: Blank page every time — no template or structure
No guidance on what sections to include
Every doctor's report looked completely different
afterBullets: General Scan, Implant Planning, Crown Prep and Follow-up templates
Each template pre-seeds relevant sections for the procedure
Switch templates without losing edits — previous content restored

slides.label: Multi-Section Builder
slides.title: A report built from blocks, not a single text field
slides.content: The new report is composed from modular sections — Image, Before/After, Cost Summary, Clinical Details — each with its own dedicated editor. Sections can be reordered, duplicated, or removed.
bullets2: Image — clinical photo with title, notes, and tooth tags
Before / After — side-by-side comparison with custom labels
Summary — itemized cost table with live totals
Clinical details — diagnosis, treatment, estimated cost, date
slides.highlight: Drag handles let doctors reorder sections to match the narrative they want to tell.

image.caption: Multi-section builder with add section panel

slides.title: Findings
slides.subtitle: Giving doctors the tools to show patients exactly what they're looking at.

slides.label: Image Annotation
slides.title: Doctors need to show, not just describe

beforeImage.caption: Old — plain image with no annotation capability

afterImage.caption: New — annotation canvas with pen, text, color picker

slides.beforeDescription: Doctors had no way to mark up images — findings had to be described in text, disconnected from the visual.
slides.afterDescription: A full annotation canvas lets doctors draw directly on images with pen, text, and color tools — then save back to the report.
beforeBullets: No annotation — doctors described findings in a separate text field
No visual connection between the explanation and the image
Patients couldn't tell what they were supposed to look at
afterBullets: Pen, arrow, and text tools with adjustable size and color
Lightbox view for full-size annotation before saving
Annotations saved with the image — no separate layer to manage

slides.label: Tooth Chart
slides.title: Tagging findings to specific teeth, not just descriptions

beforeImage.caption: Old — no tooth-level context in report

afterImage.caption: New — interactive FDI tooth chart with multi-select

slides.beforeDescription: There was no way to associate a finding with a specific tooth — everything was implied or written out in text.
slides.afterDescription: An interactive full-mouth FDI chart lets doctors tag each section to the exact teeth involved. Selected teeth appear as visible tags in the report preview.
beforeBullets: No tooth-level context — findings referenced teeth by description only
Patients had no visual reference for which teeth were affected
afterBullets: Full-mouth FDI chart with both arches in a single view
Click to select, hover preview, multiple selections per section
Selected teeth flow into the report as visible numbered tags

slides.title: Output
slides.subtitle: A report patients can actually read, understand, and trust.

slides.label: Before / After
slides.title: Showing patients what changed

afterImage.caption: New — before/after block with custom labels and comparison notes

slides.beforeDescription: No way to show treatment progression — patients couldn't see the difference between visits.
slides.afterDescription: A dedicated before/after section lets doctors place two images side by side with custom labels and comparison notes.
beforeBullets: No before/after support — progress had to be described in text
Patients left appointments without visible evidence of change
afterBullets: Side-by-side comparison with Before / After labels
Optional notes field explains what changed between the two images

slides.label: Share & Export
slides.title: A report patients can open on any device
slides.content: The old report output was a PDF that doctors had to print or email manually. The new system generates a shareable link instantly — with QR code, WhatsApp, SMS, and email options built in. An optional PIN ensures only the patient can open it.
bullets2: QR code for in-chair handoff without contact exchange
WhatsApp, SMS, and email native pickers
Optional 4–6 digit PIN — shown in share modal for verbal handover
slides.highlight: PDF export captures the full preview with consistent typography — no layout shifts, no raw images.

image.caption: Share modal with QR code and multi-channel options

outcomes.title: Report creation
outcomes.description: Templates and pre-seeded sections replaced blank-page setup — reports that took 15+ minutes now start in seconds.

outcomes.title: Clinical communication
outcomes.description: Annotation and tooth tagging gave doctors visual tools to explain findings — patients left with a document they could actually read.

outcomes.title: Report completion rate
outcomes.description: With structure provided upfront, doctors stopped skipping the report step entirely.

outcomes.title: Reach per report
outcomes.description: Multi-channel sharing meant reports reached patients via their preferred channel — not just a printed handout.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.subtitle: Designing clinical tools that work at the speed of care.
